description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Full team information: team name and city, conference and division, and colors. Also contains basic fantasy info such as IDs as well as full stadium data. This endpoint returns the teams currently associated with a competition's active season; use Team Profiles - by Competition for all-time (e.g. teams since relegated from the EPL.)

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Home and away teams, date and time, season type and week etc. are included. Also includes gameday information. This includes full stadium information (capacity, lat/long, surface etc.), top-line betting information (spread, moneyline, total), weather conditions, and broadcast information.

Examples: 2020, 2021, etc.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

(Required) Year of the season
Examples: 2020, 2021, etc.

item.name: Team Game Stats - by Date [Live & Final]

request.name: Team Game Stats - by Date [Live & Final]

description.content: Returns the box score statistical record team-wide (aggregated from all players) for all games on a given date within a competition, both live and post-game.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

(Required) The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2017-02-27, 2017-09-01.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

(Required) The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2017-02-27, 2017-09-01.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Includes regular season standings in a given league or tournament and season.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

(Required) Year of the season
Examples: 2020, 2021, etc.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

(Required) Year of the season
Examples: 2020, 2021, etc.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

(Required) The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2017-02-27, 2017-09-01.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

This method returns all box scores for a given date in a given competition, but only returns player stats that have changed in the last X minutes as specified in your API call. By definition this is a live endpoint, not final.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

(Required) The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2017-02-27, 2017-09-01.
(Required) Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are: 1, 2 ... all.

response.name: Success

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

(Required) The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2017-02-27, 2017-09-01.
(Required) Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are: 1, 2 ... all.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Full statistical information for a specified game, down to the team and player stat level, delivered live during the game, called per individual game.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.
(Required) The GameID of a Soccer game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 702, 1274, etc.

response.name: Success

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.
(Required) The GameID of a Soccer game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 702, 1274, etc.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Player Game Stats - by Date [Live & Final]

request.name: Player Game Stats - by Date [Live & Final]

description.content: Returns the box score statistical record for all involved players across all teams' games on a given date in a given competition, both live and after each game has concluded.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

(Required) The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2017-02-27, 2017-09-01.

response.name: Success

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) The date of the game(s).

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

(Required) The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2017-02-27, 2017-09-01.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Returns all season-long stats (i.e. the season total, not each individual game record) for all players for a given season.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

(Required) Year of the season
Examples: 2020, 2021, etc.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

(Required) Year of the season
Examples: 2020, 2021, etc.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Full statistical information for a specified game, down to the team and player stat level, delivered after the game is complete.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.
(Required) The GameID of a Soccer game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 702, 1274, etc.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Full statistical information for a specified date for each game that took place, down to the team and player stat level, delivered after the game is complete.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

(Required) The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2017-02-27, 2017-09-01.

response.name: Success

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Returns the non-full-game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for games on a given date. Non-full-game means 1st-half or 1st-quarter, for example, rather than full game. Returns the full line movement for the given game within a competition. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line.

Examples: 2020, 2021, etc.

(Required) Year of the season
Examples: 2020, 2021, etc.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Returns full line movement for a given BettingMarket. Due to the sheer size of the output and the level of detail, it is intended for historical data purposes and not for the most up-to-the-second lines.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.
(Required) The GameID of the desired game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets).

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Returns Markets and available outcomes for a given event (within a competition) and market type requested. A lighter call than by BettingEventID as it only includes markets tagged with the specific MarketType, a full list of which is available for each sport in its Betting Metadata endpoint.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.
(Required) The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets).
(Required) The Market Type ID of the desired MarketTypes to pull. Some common types include: 1 for Game Lines, 2 for Player Props, 3 for Team Props, 6 for Game Props

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

This works in the same way as Betting Markets by Market Type but is prefiltered to the Player Props type only. Ideal if your application will only ever require Player Props, but if you also want Team Props etc. it is recommended to use the by Market Type endpoint.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.
(Required) The unique GameID of the game in question.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Returns the non-full-game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for games on a given date. Non-full-game means 1st-half or 1st-quarter, for example, rather than full game. Returns the full line movement for the given game within a competition. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Returns in-play game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for games on a given date in a given competition. Only returns the most recently seen odds, not inclusive of line movement. As this is in-game, it will only return results while the game is in progress.

(Required) The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2018-06-20, 2018-06-23.

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds Line Movement [Sportsbook Group]

request.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds Line Movement [Sportsbook Group]

description.content: Returns the game odds for a given game in a given competition. In this endpoint both full-game and partial-game odds are included. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.
(Required) The GameID of a Soccer game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 14060, 14061, etc.

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100
(Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.
(Required) The GameID of a Soccer game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 14060, 14061, etc.

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

item.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

request.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

description.content: Returns the game odds for a given game and competition. In this endpoint both full-game and partial-game odds are included. This also includes Resulting: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

item.name: In-Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

request.name: In-Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

description.content: Returns in-play game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for a given game in a competition. This also includes Resulting: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

request.name: Betting Markets - by Market Type [Sportsbook Group]

description.content: Returns Markets and available outcomes for a given event and market type requested. A lighter call than by BettingEventID as it only includes markets tagged with the specific MarketType, a full list of which is available for each sport in its Betting Metadata endpoint. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

(Required) The Market Type ID of the desired MarketTypes to pull. Some common types include: 1 for Game Lines, 2 for Player Props, 3 for Team Props, 6 for Game Props

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.
(Required) The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets).
(Required) The Market Type ID of the desired MarketTypes to pull. Some common types include: 1 for Game Lines, 2 for Player Props, 3 for Team Props, 6 for Game Props

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Betting Player Props - by Game [Sportsbook Group]

request.name: Betting Player Props - by Game [Sportsbook Group]

description.content: This works in the same way as Betting Markets by Market Type but is prefiltered to the Player Props type only. Ideal if your application will only ever require Player Props, but if you also want Team Props etc. it is recommended to use the by Market Type endpoint. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.
(Required) The unique GameID of the game in question.

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

response.name: Success
